work_unit: SP-SCR-M-005: xhs_keepalive.py HEALTH_LOG non-atomic read-append-write race condition
situation: save_health() in scripts/xhs_keepalive.py reads .xhs_health.json, appends a record, then writes back via Path.write_text(). Under concurrent cron invocations, two processes can read the same state simultaneously, each append independently, and the last writer wins — silently dropping entries. Additionally, write_text() is not atomic: a crash or kill mid-write leaves a truncated/corrupt JSON file that subsequent reads fail to parse (caught by JSONDecodeError handler, but data is lost). This mirrors SP-SCR-H-002 (.env non-atomic rewrite) which was already fixed in this worktree with tempfile+os.replace.
action: Replaced the single HEALTH_LOG.write_text() call with the same tempfile+fsync+os.replace pattern already used by update_env_cookie() in the same file (SP-SCR-H-002 fix). Write payload to tempfile in same directory, fsync for durability, os.replace for POSIX atomic rename. Cleanup tmp file on any failure including KeyboardInterrupt. Also rejected SP-SCR-M-004 (social-scraper.js require('fs')) as false positive — file is pure ESM with no require() calls anywhere.
outcome_evidence: git commit 7eb0c64 on auto/opt-scripts-1783396445; pushed to origin (eb5c0d7..7eb0c64); python3 -c ast.parse confirms syntax valid; diff shows +20/-2 lines replacing write_text with tempfile+os.replace pattern
next_suggestion: SP-SCR-M-006: publish-xhs.js fetch API calls have no timeout (next pending route-fix from audit-2026-07-08-046)
SP-SCR-M-009: post_xhs_browseruse.py post record file non-atomic write
SP-SCR-L-002: wanxia-mail-push.sh MAIL_TO unbound variable under set -u

audit_findings.finding: HEALTH_LOG.write_text() is non-atomic read-append-write. Concurrent cron invocations race: last writer wins, silently dropping health records. Crash mid-write corrupts JSON.
audit_findings.remediation: Write to tempfile in same dir + fsync + os.replace for POSIX atomic rename. Pattern already proven in update_env_cookie() (SP-SCR-H-002).

_derived_fix_note: SP-SCR-M-005 → merged (this case). SP-SCR-M-004 → rejected (false positive: social-scraper.js has no require('fs'), file is pure ESM).
